description: Simple task with explicit 'no thinking' instruction — does the model produce visible content or only invisible tokens?

messages.content: You are a concise coding assistant. Do not think before responding. Do not reason aloud. Make tool calls directly without preamble.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command; returns stdout+stderr.

expect.notes: Target quirk: models with always-on thinking baked into weights (QwQ-32B, Devstral Small 2 current weights) cannot suppress thinking regardless of instruction. Two distinct failure modes: (a) content field is empty/null but completion_tokens >> 0 — thinking consumed all tokens, zero visible output (Devstral with --reasoning off); (b) <think>...</think> block appears in content despite suppression instruction. Compare completion_tokens to visible content length: a large ratio (>10:1) indicates hidden thinking overhead. The trivial task (pwd) minimizes reasoning need, isolating the always-on behavior from task complexity.
